pattern: With at most three neighbours per garden and four colours available, a single greedy pass that gives each garden the lowest colour none of its neighbours already use always succeeds.
hints: Each garden touches at most three others, but you have four flower types — so one choice is always free.
Build the adjacency lists, then visit gardens in order assigning whatever colour the already-decided neighbours have not taken.
For garden i, collect the colours of its neighbours and pick the smallest of {1,2,3,4} not in that set; no backtracking is ever needed.

edge_inputs.description: complete graph on four uses all four colours
many gardens, no edges at all
a star where the centre has three neighbours

stress.description: random graph respecting the max-degree-3 rule
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    deg = [0] * (n + 1)
    edges = set()
    paths = []
    attempts = n * 4
    for _ in range(attempts):
        a = rng.randint(1, n)
        b = rng.randint(1, n)
        if a == b:
            continue
        key = (min(a, b), max(a, b))
        if key in edges:
            continue
        if deg[a] >= 3 or deg[b] >= 3:
            continue
        edges.add(key)
        deg[a] += 1
        deg[b] += 1
        paths.append([a, b])
    return [n, paths]
stress.note: enforces at most 3 paths per garden so an answer always exists
